gameFeature.title: Honeyglow Woods Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods is a cozy life-sim Adventure Pack centered on a Winnie the Pooh story, Friendship Quests, beekeeping, Golden Honey crafting, and restoring four charming new areas around the Everoak Tree.

honeyglowReleaseDatePrice.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods Release Date, Price, and Platforms
honeyglowReleaseDatePrice.intro: Honeyglow Woods is the first Adventure Pack for Disney Dreamlight Valley. It launched on July 8, 2026 as a paid add-on that adds a Winnie the Pooh-themed story, three new villagers, four new areas, beekeeping, Golden Honey crafting, Pooh Sticks, and Moonstones depending on the edition you choose.

items.note: Available now across all supported Disney Dreamlight Valley platforms.
The standalone Adventure Pack requires the Disney Dreamlight Valley base game to play.
Moonstones can be spent on Premium Shop items such as clothing and furniture.

items.details: Base game + Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack
items.note: The best fit for new players who do not already own Disney Dreamlight Valley.
Platform stores may show local pricing or temporary launch discounts.
This bundle includes both the base game and the Adventure Pack.

items.details: Required for the standalone Adventure Pack
items.note: The Honeyglow Woods Edition includes the base game, so new players do not need a separate purchase.

items.details: Not included with Xbox Game Pass
items.note: Game Pass subscribers can access the base game and free updates, but the Adventure Pack is purchased separately.

items.details: Included at launch at no extra cost
items.note: Apple Arcade Edition owners receive Honeyglow Woods content without a separate Adventure Pack purchase.

honeyglowCodesAndRewards.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods Codes, Twitch Drops, and Rewards
honeyglowCodesAndRewards.intro: Honeyglow Woods launch rewards are delivered through official in-game mailbox rewards, Twitch Drops, and the standard redemption-code system. Each reward is claimed through the exact official method tied to its campaign, and successful redemptions arrive in your in-game mailbox.

rewards.name: Rainy Day Winnie the Pooh Dream Style
rewards.reward: Exclusive Winnie the Pooh Dream Style
rewards.delivery: Delivered directly to the in-game mailbox

rewards.name: Piglet's Clothesline
rewards.claim: Link your Twitch and Disney Dreamlight Valley accounts, then watch participating streams for 60 minutes
rewards.delivery: Claim in the Twitch Drops Inventory, then redeem from the in-game mailbox
rewards.availability: July 8, 2026 10:00 AM EDT to July 15, 2026 9:59 AM EDT

rewards.name: Standard Redemption Code Path
rewards.reward: Eligible in-game items or currency attached to the code
rewards.claim: Open Settings, open the Help tab, enter the code exactly, then select Claim
rewards.delivery: Successful code rewards are sent to the in-game mailbox
rewards.availability: Available whenever Gameloft releases active codes

rewards.name: Mac and Apple Arcade Support
rewards.code: Redemption-code entry is not available on Mac or Apple Arcade Edition
rewards.reward: Eligible code contents handled through Customer Care
rewards.claim: Contact Gameloft Customer Care for code-content eligibility on these platforms
rewards.availability: Applies to Mac and Apple Arcade Edition players

honeyglowBeginnerGuide.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods Beginner Guide
honeyglowBeginnerGuide.intro: Honeyglow Woods begins with a mysterious mailbox package and a Tree Sprout that grows into a magical gateway. After stepping through, your first goal is to meet Winnie the Pooh and start clearing the strange fog. This walkthrough covers the first playable steps, from accessing the Adventure Pack to entering the Woods and starting the opening story.

steps.title: Own or Access Honeyglow Woods
steps.description: Buy the standalone Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack if you already own Disney Dreamlight Valley, or choose the Honeyglow Woods Edition if you are starting fresh with the base game included.
steps.tip: Apple Arcade Edition owners receive Honeyglow Woods content at launch without a separate purchase.

steps.title: Check Your Mailbox Package
steps.description: The adventure begins with a mysterious package in your in-game mailbox containing childhood keepsakes and a Tree Sprout.
steps.tip: This package opens the path to the Honeyglow Woods gateway.

steps.title: Plant the Tree Sprout
steps.description: Plant the Tree Sprout and watch it grow into a magical glowing tree gateway connected to Honeyglow Woods.
steps.tip: The gateway is your entrance into the new Adventure Pack area.

steps.title: Place the Magical Gateway
steps.description: Place the glowing tree gateway anywhere in your Valley or on a base game Floating Island.
steps.tip: The gateway can be moved or removed later, so you can adjust its location as your Valley grows.

steps.title: Enter Honeyglow Woods
steps.description: Step through the gateway to reach Honeyglow Woods, where a strange fog has separated the residents and changed the land.
steps.tip: Special portals inside the Woods connect back to Scrooge McDuck's Store and Chez Remy.

steps.title: Meet Winnie the Pooh First
steps.description: Start the opening story by meeting Winnie the Pooh, then work with him to uncover clues, clear fog, and restore warmth to the Woods.
steps.tip: The story then leads you toward Eeyore and Piglet as you progress.

steps.title: Explore the Four New Areas
steps.description: Move through Drowsybloom Acres, the Gloommeadow, Braveheart Grove, and the Nectar Apiary while solving the mystery of the Everoak Tree.
steps.tip: Each area has its own mood, secrets, and restoration goals.

steps.title: Start Beekeeping and Golden Honey Crafting
steps.description: Restore Busy Bees' Houses, place flowers nearby, gather Golden Honey, and use it for new recipes, furniture, and decorations.
steps.tip: Busy Bees' Houses can be moved to other biomes, including the base game Valley.

honeyglowCharactersGuide.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods Characters Guide
honeyglowCharactersGuide.intro: Honeyglow Woods adds three headline Winnie the Pooh villagers: Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet. Each one arrives with a home, Friendship Quests, and Friendship Rewards, and unlocking them expands your Valley with new character homes, themed rewards, and story moments tied to the fog, honey, and the Everoak Tree.

characters.name: Winnie the Pooh
characters.role: First companion in the Honeyglow Woods story
characters.story: Pooh helps you begin uncovering clues, clearing the strange fog, and restoring the warmth of the Woods.
characters.progression: Invite Pooh back to the Valley with his own home, Friendship Quests, and Friendship Rewards.
characters.whyUnlock: Pooh is the entry point for the Adventure Pack story and the character closest to the early Honeyglow Woods mystery.
characters.moments: Hide-and-seek, cloud-watching, Pooh Sticks, and the Rainy Day Winnie the Pooh Dream Style early adopter bonus.

characters.name: Eeyore
characters.role: Second major friend to find after Pooh
characters.story: Eeyore is separated in the Woods and needs help searching for his missing tail while the land is still touched by gloom and fog.
characters.progression: Invite Eeyore back to the Valley with his own home, Friendship Quests, and Friendship Rewards.
characters.whyUnlock: Eeyore deepens the story and connects strongly to the calm, reflective mood of the Gloommeadow.
characters.moments: Missing-tail story beats, quiet friendship moments, and progress through the fog-covered areas.

characters.name: Piglet
characters.role: Final headline Winnie the Pooh character on the unlock path
characters.story: Piglet's story centers on finding courage as the Woods are slowly restored.
characters.progression: Invite Piglet back to the Valley with his own home, Friendship Quests, and Friendship Rewards.
characters.whyUnlock: Piglet completes the main Winnie the Pooh trio for Honeyglow Woods and adds more friendship progression and themed rewards.
characters.moments: Courage-focused quests, friendship activities, and the Piglet's Clothesline Twitch Drop reward theme.

faq.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods.

questions.question: What is Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods?
questions.answer: Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods is the first Adventure Pack for Disney Dreamlight Valley, launched on July 8, 2026. It adds a Winnie the Pooh-themed story with Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet, four new areas, beekeeping, Golden Honey crafting, and the Pooh Sticks mini-game.

questions.question: How much does Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods cost?
questions.answer: The standalone Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack costs US$16.99 and requires the base game. The Honeyglow Woods Edition bundle, which includes the base game and the Adventure Pack, costs US$49.99.

questions.question: Do I need the base game to play Honeyglow Woods?
questions.answer: The standalone Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack requires the Disney Dreamlight Valley base game. New players can buy the Honeyglow Woods Edition, which includes both the base game and the Adventure Pack, so no separate base-game purchase is needed.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods?
questions.answer: Open Disney Dreamlight Valley, go to Settings, open the Help tab, enter the official code exactly, then select Claim. Successful redemptions are sent to your in-game mailbox. Redemption-code entry is not available on Mac or Apple Arcade Edition.

questions.question: Who are the new characters in Honeyglow Woods?
questions.answer: Honeyglow Woods adds three Winnie the Pooh villagers — Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet — each with a home, Friendship Quests, and Friendship Rewards you can bring back to your Valley.

questions.question: What is Golden Honey in Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods?
questions.answer: Golden Honey is the main new resource in Honeyglow Woods, produced by Busy Bees' Houses when they are surrounded by flowers. It powers new recipes, Honeyglow furniture, and decorations across your Valley.
